action: Verdict-FAIL cycles discard their builder work: the orchestrator prunes the cycle worktree on abnormal end, and FAIL cycles commit NOTHING to their cycle branch (confirmed: cycles 306/307 branches had zero commits over main; ADR-0046 Layer 2 was BUILT by the loop twice [306, 307] and LOST twice, forcing interactive reimplementation). The recoverable/abort paths already PRESERVE worktrees (log: 'preserving worktree ... recover via evolve loop --resume or evolve cycle reset') for SOME terminal classes — extend that to the verdict-FAIL terminal so a near-miss cycle's diff is salvageable. Design: on FinalVerdict=FAIL, before prune, commit the worktree's working tree to the cycle branch as a WIP commit (clearly marked non-shippable, e.g. 'wip(cycle-N): FAILED audit — preserved for salvage') OR leave the worktree on disk like the abort path. Operator can then cherry-pick/audit the salvageable parts instead of the loop rebuilding from scratch. Interacts with EVOLVE_LOOP_MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILS (continue-on-fail): when continuing, the FAILED cycle's worktree must be preserved BEFORE the next cycle provisions, without leaking into the next cycle's tree. TDD: a FAIL cycle leaves either a recoverable worktree or a WIP cycle-branch commit; a PASS cycle is unaffected; continue-on-fail preserves then cleanly starts the next cycle.
evidence_pointer: go/internal/core/orchestrator.go (worktree prune on abnormal end; the 'preserving worktree' path); cycles 306/307 zero-commit branches; EVOLVE_LOOP_MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILS interaction (cmd_loop.go)
